## Union & Cross-Indication Noise

```
Patient Cohort = patients(Q1) ∪ patients(Q2) ∪ patients(Q3)
```

**Known false-positive contributors in Q2/Q3:**

| Indication | Antibiotic Overlap |
|---|---|
| Urinary tract infection (UTI) | Ciprofloxacin monotherapy (no metronidazole) |
| *C. difficile* | Metronidazole monotherapy (no ciprofloxacin) |
| Intra-abdominal abscess / peritonitis | Both antibiotics (high diverticulitis PPV) |
| Pelvic inflammatory disease (PID) | Both antibiotics (moderate noise) |

**Noise-reduction refinement strategy:** Intersect `patients(Q2) ∩ patients(Q3)` — patients prescribed **both** ciprofloxacin **and** metronidazole simultaneously have a much higher prior probability of diverticulitis treatment vs. UTI or *C. diff* (where only one drug is used). Co-prescribing within the same encounter date window (`authoredon` overlap) is a strong empirical signal for Path C diverticulitis.
